issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle function updates CSS classes to show/hide the menu but fails to update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Additionally, the state is not initialized to "false" on page load, leaving screen reader users unaware of the menu's toggleable nature and current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`; Line 1466: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`; Line 1515: `function init() { ... _initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller manages the visibility of submenus but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons when they are opened or closed. Without this programmatic state update, assistive technology users cannot perceive whether the sub-navigation is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1595: `function _openInstance(inst) { if (inst.open) return; inst.open = true; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`; Line 1608: `function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { if (!inst.open) return; inst.open = false; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`; Line 1695: `function init() { ... if (trigger && panel) { register(trigger, panel); setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); }`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion's open function animates the panel and updates CSS classes but fails to set aria-expanded to "true" on the trigger button. While the close function correctly resets the state, the initial expansion is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2548: `function open(item, announceToSR) { if (!isPlainObject(item) || item.open) return; item.open = true; setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox expands a suggestion listbox but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the input element. Screen reader users will not be notified that a list of suggestions has appeared or disappeared in response to their typing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2063: `function _open() { if (_searchState.open) return; _searchState.open = true; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`; Line 2073: `function _close() { if (!_searchState.open) return; _searchState.open = false; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`; Line 2145: `function init(opts) { ... const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl); setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons function as a group of toggle controls but only update visual CSS classes. They lack the aria-pressed attribute to communicate which billing cycle is currently active to non-sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, breaking navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 632: min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search button has width: 28px and height: 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target. Small interactive areas are difficult for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and overflow: visible, making content visible on screen. Screen reader users will hear duplicated content, while sighted users see hidden text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has transform transitions without a reduced-motion override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience dizziness or nausea from unexpected motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card selector uses transform: translateY(-2px) on hover without a reduced-motion override. This can trigger motion sickness for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card selector uses transform: translateY(-3px) on hover without a reduced-motion override. Unexpected movement can disorient users with motion sensitivities.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability. Users with dyslexia or low vision may struggle to distinguish word boundaries.
issuesFound.fix: Line 154: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.32:1
issuesFound.explanation: --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background has contrast ratio of 2.32:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. Low-vision users cannot read this text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 40: --colour-text-secondary: #666666;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: All links have text-decoration: none with no alternative visual indicator. Users with colour blindness cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 160: a { text-decoration: underline; }

fpTitles: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, severely hindering navigation and access to interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line where :focus-visible is defined: Change "outline: none;" to "outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);"

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button due to the small interactive area.
issuesFound.fix: Line where .btn-icon is defined: Change "min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px;" to "min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;" (or to 44px for better compliance).

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static, clip: auto, and other properties that do not hide content visually but instead expose it. This breaks the intended functionality of hiding content from sighted users while keeping it accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line where .sr-only is defined: Replace the entire rule with "position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0;"

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img has transform and transition animations on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for it is empty, so the motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion. This can cause discomfort for users with motion sensitivities.
issuesFound.fix: In the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img, add "transition: none; transform: none;"

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words, making text harder to read, especially for users with dyslexia or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line where body is defined: Change "word-spacing: -0.2em;" to "word-spacing: normal;"

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule for a elements sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without underlines or another non-colour visual differentiator, links may be hard to distinguish from surrounding text for colour-blind users or when colour contrast is low.
issuesFound.fix: Line where a is defined: Change "text-decoration: none;" to "text-decoration: underline;"

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size for #site-search button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button has width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. This can make it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the button accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line where #site-search button is defined: Change "width: 28px; height: 28px;" to "width: 44px; height: 44px;"

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible and :focus:not(:focus-visible) selectors set outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); } Line 254: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, which does not hide content visually. Screen reader users may encounter duplicate announcements, while sighted users see hidden text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 272: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has a transform transition without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort from unnecessary animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 624: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 40: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. Users with low vision cannot read the text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 15: --colour-text-secondary: #666666;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none globally, removing underlines from all links. Users with colour blindness may not distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 44: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The month/year toggle switch (data-billing-toggle) does not set aria-pressed on the switch element. Screen reader users cannot perceive the toggle state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, after toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear), add: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', isYear ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the updated product count and state are not announced to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the results have changed, degrading experience (WCAG 4.1.3).
issuesFound.fix: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, add: liveRegion.announce(`Filter applied. Showing ${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''}.`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods (monthly/annual) updates prices visually but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology may miss the update, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod, after emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...), add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()} billing.`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, aria-invalid="true" is not set on the input. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, inside the else block (invalid), add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); In the valid block, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded="false" set on page load when the menu is closed. Screen reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after setting _trigger and _drawer, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded="false" set initially when their panels are hidden. Screen reader users cannot perceive the collapsed state on page load, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register, after panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''), add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open, mobileNav.close, and mobileNav.toggle functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2235: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2253: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`. Line 2269: Update toggle to set based on new state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the associated trigger buttons. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the expanded state of dropdown menus, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2405: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2420: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function toggles the active billing period button visually but does not set aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is selected, failing WCAG 4.1.2 for toggle button state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3835: Inside the forEach, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product count but does not announce this change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change, degrading experience per WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) : \`${totalMatches} products found\`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function changes the billing period and updates prices but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users who rely on announcements will not know the pricing display has updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3810: After `_pricingState.period = period;`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users will not hear the invalid state announced, breaking WCAG 4.1.2 for form error indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5000: In the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`. In the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation aria-expanded not initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set an initial aria-expanded value on the trigger button. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state of the menu, failing WCAG 4.1.2 for initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300: After `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu aria-expanded not initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init function registers dropdown triggers but does not set initial aria-expanded="false". Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state of dropdowns, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: In the register function, after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open() and close() functions toggle visibility but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3000: In open(), add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');
Line 3010: In close(), add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open and close functions do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3200: In _openInstance(inst), add: inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');
Line 3210: In _closeInstance(inst), add: inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function marks fields as invalid visually but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4500: In the else block, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); In the if (result.valid) block, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (role="switch") changes state but does not update aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive the current toggle state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4100: In _syncToggleState(period), for the toggle switch element (_toggleEl), add: _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen readers may not know the control's initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: In init(), after getting _trigger, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. This violates ARIA state initialization requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3300: In init(), after registering each dropdown instance, add: inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: contact form fields lack autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields (name, email, etc.) do not have autocomplete attributes. This hinders users with cognitive disabilities and password managers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4800: In init(), after binding validation, add autocomplete attributes: e.g., for name field: field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name'); for email: field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button click handler lacks keyboard event support
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop button has a click event listener but no keydown handler for Enter/Space. Keyboard-only users cannot activate the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2900: Add keydown event listener: _btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') _onClick(e); });

fpTitles: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
contact form fields lack autocomplete attributes
scrollToTop button click handler lacks keyboard event support

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown component's trigger button controls a submenu but lacks aria-expanded to communicate its open/closed state to screen reader users. This prevents users of assistive technology from knowing if the dropdown is currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1392: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button controls a collapsible panel but does not have aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot know if the answer is currently visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The product category filter tabs are toggle buttons that visually show active state but lack aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline (`outline: none`) without providing any visible replacement such as a box‑shadow or colored border. Keyboard users lose a clear focus indicator on all interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: `:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their outline on focus because `.btn:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` and only changes the offset. No alternative visual cue is provided, making keyboard navigation invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 310: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes its outline (`outline: none`) without any replacement, leaving focus invisible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the outline on focus and do not supply a visible alternative, breaking focus visibility for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 460: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons hide the outline (`outline: none`) and only shift the offset, providing no visible focus style.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes its outline on focus without any replacement, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which region is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `.table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Links that cover an entire feature card lose their outline on focus and only change offset, leaving no visible focus cue.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the outline without providing an alternative visual focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 840: `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links hide their outline on focus and do not supply any other visible focus style.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the outline on focus without any replacement, breaking focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 910: `.footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Links inside the footer navigation lose their outline on focus and only offset the outline, providing no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 940: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn (min-height 32px)
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have a minimum height of 32 px, which is below the WCAG‑recommended 44 px target size, making them hard to tap for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `.btn { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-sm (min-height 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: Small variant buttons are only 20 px tall, far below the 44 px minimum, creating a difficult tap target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-lg (min-height 36px)
issuesFound.explanation: Large buttons are 36 px high, still below the 44 px minimum recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: `.btn-lg { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-icon (20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons are only 20 px square, which is well under the 44 px minimum target area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .nav-toggle (28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button is 28 px high and wide, below the 44 px minimum, making it hard to activate on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 330: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on filter tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons have a minimum height of 28 px, which does not meet the 44 px minimum target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 460: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons are only 28 px tall, below the recommended 44 px minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 840: `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on social‑link anchors
issuesFound.explanation: Social media link anchors have a minimum height of 28 px, which is less than the 44 px target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: `.social-links a { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can compress words and impair readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: `body { word-spacing: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual differentiator (border, background, font‑weight) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 140: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have a transition that includes `transform` (used on `:active` to translateY), but there is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule that disables this animation, violating reduced‑motion requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 300: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:focus-visible, .btn:active { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` has a transition on `transform` and a hover state that rotates the image, but the existing reduced‑motion media block does not disable the transition, leaving motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 730: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Button transition includes transform without reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: .btn defines a transition on transform but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override disabling or simplifying the animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 340: .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); } → add reduced‑motion guard:

Line 340: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform transition without reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img has a transition: transform that animates rotation and perspective, but no reduced‑motion media query disables it, breaching WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 560: .hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); } → add:

Line 560: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Feature‑card hover transform without reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: .feature-card:hover applies transform: translateY(-2px) with a transition, but no reduced‑motion override is provided, violating WCAG 2.3.3.

Line 620: .feature-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); } → add:

Line 620: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.fix:
Line 720: .pricing-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); } → add:

issuesFound.title: Pricing‑card featured hover transform without reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: .pricing-card--featured:hover applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) with a transition, but no reduced‑motion media query disables it, violating WCAG 2.3.3.

fpTitles: Hero image hover transform animation lacks reduced‑motion guard

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions add/remove CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never set or update the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the navigation drawer is open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) is created in the markup without an `aria-expanded` attribute, and no script sets it on initialisation. Assistive technologies therefore receive no information about the default closed state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria‑expanded when opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` the panel visibility is toggled, but the corresponding trigger button never receives an updated `aria-expanded` value. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded state on load
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons with `aria-haspopup="true"` are created without an explicit `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, leaving the initial collapsed state undefined for AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are parsed, the `trigger` receives `aria-controls` but no `aria-expanded` attribute, so the default collapsed state is not communicated.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not update aria‑pressed on its button element
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) changes visual state and fires analytics, but never updates `aria-pressed` (or `aria-checked`) to reflect the current period. Screen‑reader users cannot tell which period is active.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks initial aria‑pressed state on load
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle element is created without an `aria-pressed` attribute, so its default state is unknown to AT.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult` adds error‑message classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the field (or removes it on success). Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the visible count badge (`_countBadge`) is updated, but no live‑region or ARIA announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new result count.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches billing periods, the UI updates numbers but no ARIA live region is used, so AT users receive no feedback about the change.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on the trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions of the mobile navigation only add/remove CSS classes and focus‑trap the drawer. They never set aria‑expanded on the hamburger button, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls to reference the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger element (`.nav-toggle`) is never given an aria‑controls attribute that points to the drawer element, leaving the relationship between button and menu undisclosed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element is hidden only with CSS (`hidden` class) but never receives aria‑hidden="true" in its closed state, so screen readers may still expose its content.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not expose its state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The element with [data-billing-toggle] acts as a two‑state switch (monthly ↔ annual) but never sets aria‑pressed, so AT cannot convey its current state.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The previous/next buttons (`[data-carousel-prev]` and `[data-carousel-next]`) are plain <button> elements with only visual arrows; they have no aria‑label or inner text, so they are invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
```html
<button data-carousel-prev aria-label="Previous testimonial">←</button>
<button data-carousel-next aria-label="Next testimonial">→</button>
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers are missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu.register the trigger receives aria‑haspopup but never gets an aria‑expanded attribute (default false). Without it, screen readers cannot know whether the menu is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true');
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← add
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic price changes in pricing.setPeriod are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the displayed prices are updated, but no live‑region announcement is made, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the new pricing.

fpTitles: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack accessible names

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.toggle does not update aria‑expanded on the hamburger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle()` function only opens or closes the mobile navigation drawer but never reflects the new state on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`). Assistive technologies therefore cannot know whether the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a dropdown trigger toggles the panel visibility, but the trigger’s `aria-expanded` state is never updated, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria‑expanded on the FAQ trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened the panel becomes visible, but the associated button’s `aria-expanded` attribute remains unchanged, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle button does not update aria‑pressed when switching billing period
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`data-billing-toggle`) changes the pricing display but never updates `aria-pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which period is active.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers lack an initial aria‑expanded="false" attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is rendered closed, its trigger button does not have `aria-expanded="false"` set, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern on page load.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The validator adds error messages but never toggles the `aria-invalid` state, so assistive technologies cannot detect invalid inputs.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce result count to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is updated visually but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of how many items match.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.toggle does not set aria‑controls on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button controls the navigation drawer but lacks an `aria-controls` reference, breaking the relationship between trigger and controlled region.

issuesFound.title: Form <label> missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The <FormGroup> component renders a <label> without an htmlFor attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot associate the label with its input. All form fields that use <FormGroup> (e.g., the newsletter email field) are affected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1023: `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`  

---

issuesFound.title: Form field missing accessible label (htmlFor)
issuesFound.explanation: Each <TextInput> or <Textarea> inside a <FormGroup> has no programmatically associated <label> because <FormGroup> doesn’t pass htmlFor to the <label>. Users of assistive technology cannot identify the purpose of the inputs (newsletter, contact form fields).
issuesFound.fix:
Add htmlFor to the <label> in <FormGroup> as in Issue 1 (line 1023).  

---

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria‑invalid when invalid state is set
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> and <Textarea> components receive an invalid prop but never render aria‑invalid, so screen readers are not informed when a field is in error. All fields that can become invalid (newsletter email, contact name, email, subject, message) are affected.
issuesFound.fix:
In TextInput and Textarea components, add `aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined}` to the returned <input>/<textarea>.  

---

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria‑describedby (broken reference)
issuesFound.explanation: Each input sets aria-describedby to an ID that does not exist (e.g., ‘newsletter-email-error’, ‘contact-name-error’). Assistive technologies cannot locate the error text.
issuesFound.fix: Create matching … or use ErrorMessage with the same ID, and ensure the ID exists for each field. ---

issuesFound.title: Required‑field asterisk not aria‑hidden (announces bare ‘*’)
issuesFound.explanation: The <span className="form-required-marker"> contains only “*” and is not hidden from AT, causing a redundant asterisk announcement. All required fields in the contact form are affected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1025: ` *` ---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑expanded attribute (NavDropdown)
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> that opens/closes a navigation submenu uses aria-controls but does not expose its open/closed state via aria-expanded, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the submenu state. Two NavDropdown instances (Products → Solutions and Resources) are affected.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the <button> in NavDropdown (around line 735).  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded (and aria‑pressed)
issuesFound.explanation: The main mobile‑nav toggle button controls the off‑canvas panel but does not expose its state via aria-expanded or aria‑pressed, making the open/close state invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 842: `<button … aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-pressed={isOpen}>`  

---

issuesFound.title: Video play/pause button missing aria‑pressed (state)
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles background video playback changes its icon but does not indicate the pressed state with aria‑pressed, so AT cannot know whether the video is playing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 617: `<Button … aria-pressed={isPlaying} …>`  

---

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs buttons missing aria‑pressed (state)
issuesFound.explanation: Each filter‑tab button reflects the active filter via CSS but does not expose this via aria‑pressed, leaving AT users unaware of the current filter. Five buttons are affected.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to the <button> inside FilterTabs (around line 1310).

issuesFound.title: Pause button for testimonials carousel missing aria‑pressed (state)

Line 1465: `<Button … aria-pressed={isPausedByUser} …>`

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label

issuesFound.fix: Line 690: `<label for="contact-email" class="sr-only">Email address</label><input type="email" id="contact-email" name="email" autocomplete="work email" required aria-required="true">`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The "First name" and "Last name" inputs collect personal data but lack autocomplete attributes. This fails WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose), making it harder for users to fill out forms using autofill.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 680: `<input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>`
  Line 685: `<input type="text" id="contact-last-name" name="last_name" autocomplete="family-name" required aria-required="true">`

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are two <nav> elements in the header, but neither has an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. This fails WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships), making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish between them.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 40: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">`
  Line 80: `<nav aria-label="Account navigation">`

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy skips from <h2 id="how-it-works-heading"> to <h5> in the "Connect your project" step. This fails WCAG 2.4.6 (Headings and Labels), making it harder for screen reader users to understand the document structure.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 330: `

Connect your project

`

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The button with id "faq-2-question" references "faq-2-answer" in aria-controls, but the attribute is incorrectly named "aria-controls" instead of "aria-controls". This fails WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value), breaking the association for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400: `<button type="button" aria-controls="faq-2-answer" id="faq-2-question">`

issuesFound.title: Page missing title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty, which fails WCAG 2.4.2 (Page Titled). Users who rely on screen readers cannot identify the page's purpose or content without a title.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: `<title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools for every team</title>`

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element for "nova-health.svg" is missing the alt attribute entirely. Screen reader users cannot access the information conveyed by this image.
issuesFound.fix: Line 163: ``

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> elements in the first column of the comparison table lack scope attributes. While position may be unambiguous, explicitly defining scope="row" improves clarity for screen reader users navigating complex tables.

issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy skips from h1 to h3 without an intervening h2. This disrupts the document outline and can confuse screen reader users navigating by headings.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 408: `

Connect your project

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` rule removes the default focus outline without providing a visible alternative for keyboard users who rely on focus indicators to navigate. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) and 2.4.11 (Focus Appearance).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 352: Remove the rule entirely or replace with a visible focus style.
    Line 356: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` selector sets a `min-height: 20px` and `padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5)`, resulting in a touch target smaller than the 24×24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 (Target Size). This affects users with motor impairments who rely on larger touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1024: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) by potentially hiding critical content from assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` selector applies a transform animation on hover (`transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg)`) but lacks a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow the animation. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by making text harder to parse. This violates WCAG 1.4.8 (Visual Presentation).
issuesFound.fix: Line 60: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` from the `body` selector.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links, relying solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) as it fails to provide a non-colour visual indicator for links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add a non-colour differentiator (e.g., `a:hover { text-decoration: underline; border-bottom: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }`)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` rule removes the default focus outline with `outline: none` but does not provide a visible alternative focus indicator on the base selector. Users who rely on keyboard navigation will not see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 398: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`
    Line 402: Remove the `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` rule entirely.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` toggle the `is-open` class on the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, blocking keyboard and screen reader users from understanding whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10656: Add after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
    ```
    setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
    ```
    Line 10664: Add after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
    ```
    setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`) when the navigation drawer is closed on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as the initial state is not programmatically exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10680: Add after `if (!_trigger || !_drawer) {`
    ```
    setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in `pricing` toggles the billing period between "month" and "year" but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`[data-billing-toggle]`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, blocking users who rely on assistive technologies from understanding the current billing period.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12100: Add inside `_onToggleClick` after `setPeriod(next);`
    ```
    setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', next === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `pricing` does not set `aria-pressed` on the toggle button (`[data-billing-toggle]`) when the default billing period is "annual". This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as the initial state is not programmatically exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12150: Add after `setPeriod(initPeriod, false);`
    ```
    setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', initPeriod === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` change the visible state of the navigation drawer but do not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as users who rely on assistive technologies are not informed of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10656: Add after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`
    ```
    liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
    ```
    Line 10664: Add after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`
    ```
    liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` adds/removes error classes and inserts error messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as screen readers cannot announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13000: Add after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`
    ```
    setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
    ```
    Line 13010: Add after `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`
    ```
    setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` removes error classes and hides error messages for valid fields but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` toggle the `is-open` class on the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, leaving keyboard and assistive technology users unaware of the menu's open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10527: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 10535: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` change the navigation state but do not announce these changes via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers are left unaware of the menu's open/closed status, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10527: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
    Line 10535: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the initial state is not programmatically exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10560: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in `inlineValidator` identifies invalid fields but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field element. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as the error state is not programmatically exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12050: In `_renderResult`, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` when `result.valid` is `false`.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in `inlineValidator` does not clear `aria-invalid` when a field becomes valid. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 as the error state persists even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12050: In `_renderResult`, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` when `result.valid` is `true`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in `pricing` toggles the billing period but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the state change is not programmatically exposed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10350: `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(next === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `pricing` does not set an initial `aria-pressed` value on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the initial state is not programmatically exposed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10380: `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(initPeriod === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` trigger button (`_trigger`) has a click handler (`_onTriggerClick`) but no keyboard handler for `Enter` or `Space`. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as the menu cannot be opened via keyboard alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10545: Add `if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); toggle(); }` to `_onTriggerKeydown`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle` function in `mobileNav` toggles the CSS class `is-open` on the navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, leaving keyboard and assistive technology users unaware of the navigation menu's open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`) when the navigation drawer is closed on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the initial state is not programmatically determinable.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle` function in `mobileNav` toggles the navigation drawer's visibility but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as users are not informed of the navigation menu's open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in `inlineValidator` validates form fields but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid inputs. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as screen readers are not informed of validation errors.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` clears the error message for valid inputs but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 as the error state is not programmatically cleared.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in `pricing` toggles the billing period but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the ARIA state is not synchronised with the visual state.

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `pricing` does not initialise the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`) when the billing period is set on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the initial state is not programmatically determinable.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button (`_toggleEl`) in `pricing` is not a native `<button>` element and lacks a `role="button"` attribute and keyboard event handlers for Space/Enter. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) and 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as the element is not operable via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The email input field (`_emailEl`) in `newsletterForm` collects personal data (email address) but lacks an `autocomplete="email"` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose) as it prevents browsers and assistive technologies from auto-filling the field.

issuesFound.title: Focus not returned to trigger element on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in `mobileNav` closes the navigation drawer but does not return focus to the trigger button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) as keyboard users are left without a clear focus point after closing the menu.

issuesFound.title: Focus not managed on modal open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `mobileNav` opens the navigation drawer but does not move focus into the drawer. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) as keyboard users are not directed to the newly opened content.

issuesFound.title: Focus trap not activated on modal open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `mobileNav` opens the navigation drawer but does not activate a focus trap. This violates WCAG 2.1.2 (No Keyboard Trap) as keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into background content.

issuesFound.title: Focus trap not deactivated on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in `mobileNav` closes the navigation drawer but does not deactivate the focus trap. This violates WCAG 2.1.2 as the focus trap remains active even after the drawer is closed.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content injected via innerHTML without adjacent ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `render` function in `suggestionListRenderer` updates the suggestion list using `innerHTML`, which changes visible content, but there is no adjacent ARIA live region to announce the change. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as screen readers are not informed of the updated suggestions.

issuesFound.title: setTimeout/setInterval changing visible content without pause/stop control
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel` uses `setTimeout` in the `_scheduleNext` function to auto-advance slides, but there is no user-controllable pause/stop mechanism. This violates WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide) as users cannot pause the carousel.

issuesFound.title: Click handler on non-native element without matching keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle` function in `mobileNav` is triggered by a click event on the navigation toggle button (`_trigger`), but there is no matching keyboard handler for Space/Enter. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as the element is not operable via keyboard.

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing role and keyboard handler
Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
Focus trap not deactivated on modal close
setTimeout/setInterval changing visible content without pause/stop control
Click handler on non-native element without matching keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown component uses a boolean state `isOpen` to control the visibility of a submenu, but the trigger button does not receive an `aria-expanded` prop. Screen reader users cannot perceive whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed, blocking access to the submenu content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1064: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded prop
Decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The social media link icons in the SiteFooter component are decorative spans that lack aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce them as empty or generic "span" elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2803: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="social-icon" />`

issuesFound.title: Toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown component uses isOpen state to control a sub-menu but does not pass aria-expanded={isOpen} to the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware of the dropdown's state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1082: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component supports an invalid prop but does not pass aria-invalid={invalid} to the underlying input element, preventing screen readers from announcing the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1605: `<input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The BillingToggle component uses value state to control active/inactive buttons but does not pass aria-pressed to the buttons, leaving screen reader users unaware of the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2005: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}>` and Line 2010: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}>` and remove the Badge from inside the button (Badge is decorative and should be moved outside or hidden).

issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component renders a required-field marker as ` *` without aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce "asterisk" redundantly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1580: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component uses isLoading state to control loading state but does not pass aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1450: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Spinner icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component renders a spinner icon during loading but does not hide it from assistive technology with aria-hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1451: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="btn-spinner" />`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component highlights current-page links with className="nav-link--current" but does not pass aria-current="page" to the link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Repeated landmark region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SiteFooter component contains two <nav> elements (one for social links, one for footer navigation) but neither has an aria-label or aria-labelledby to distinguish them for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750: `<nav aria-label="Social media links" className="site-footer__social">` and Line 2780: `<nav aria-label="Footer navigation" className="site-footer__nav">`

issuesFound.title: Icon-only interactive element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an icon-only button without an aria-label or text content, leaving screen reader users unaware of its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1300: `<IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>` (the LABELS object already exists and provides the correct accessible name)

issuesFound.title: Form field missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The SearchInput component renders a TextInput without an explicit aria-label or visible label (the label is visually hidden but the input needs an accessible name).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1340: `<TextInput id={id} type="search" value={value} onChange={(ev) => onChange(ev.target.value)} placeholder={placeholder} autoComplete="off" aria-label="Search" className="site-search-form__input" />` (the aria-label is already present, but ensure the label element is correctly associated with htmlFor)

issuesFound.title: Required field not programmatically marked as required
issuesFound.explanation: The ContactForm component renders required fields with visual markers but does not pass aria-required="true" or the HTML required attribute to the inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: `<TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria-required="true" ... />` and similarly for other required fields (email, subject, message)

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The ContactForm component renders error messages but does not link them to the inputs via aria-describedby.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: `<TextInput id="contact-name" ... aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} />` and similarly for other fields (ensure errorId is passed to FormGroup and rendered as the id of ErrorMessage)

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a replacement color, box-shadow, or border. This renders keyboard focus invisible for any element that does not have a specific override adding a visible style, blocking keyboard-only users from tracking their position.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline but does not provide a visible alternative like a box-shadow or border color. Users navigating via keyboard will not see which button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide the content. This exposes screen-reader-only text to sighted users, cluttering the interface, and may fail to hide it from assistive technology if not implemented correctly.

issuesFound.title: Root font-size sets body text below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px. Since body uses 1rem, all body text renders at 12px, which is below the recommended 16px minimum for readability, affecting users with low vision or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 213: html { font-size: 16px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text harder to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 232: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Global link underlines removed without consistent alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. While some links have hover states, relying on color alone to distinguish links from surrounding text fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 247: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links rely on color alone for hover state
issuesFound.explanation: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover changes only the text color. Without an underline, background change, or other non-color indicator, users with color blindness may not perceive the interactive state.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in section header paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: .section-header p uses #aaaaaa text on a white/light background (#ffffff or #f8fafc). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.8:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 837: .section-header p { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in logo bar label

issuesFound.fix: Line 993: .logo-bar-label { color: var(--colour-neutral-700); }

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon sets min-width and min-height to 20px. This is below the 24px minimum (WCAG 2.5.8) and significantly below the 44px recommended target size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target below minimum size on desktop
issuesFound.explanation: #site-search button[type="submit"] is explicitly sized to 28px by 28px with no padding. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size, hindering users with dexterity issues.

issuesFound.title: Button motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform for buttons, which may cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: .skip-link uses a transform transition to slide into view. This motion is not disabled in the empty prefers-reduced-motion media query block, potentially causing issues for users who prefer reduced motion.
