issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for all interactive elements that do not have a specific override, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector explicitly removes the outline again without adding a box-shadow or border. Even if the global rule were fixed, this specific override would hide the focus indicator on all buttons.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class makes content visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is intended to hide content visually while keeping it for screen readers, but it uses position: static and width: auto, which renders the content fully visible on the page. This clutters the UI for sighted users and fails to hide sensitive or auxiliary text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on skip-link without reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses a transform transition for its entrance animation. The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 433 is empty, so users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on hero image without reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element uses a transform transition for a 3D hover effect. The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 763 is empty, failing to disable the motion for users who requested it.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which pulls words closer together. This reduces legibility, especially for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision, and contradicts WCAG best practices for text spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 266: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Root font-size below 16px reduces readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size to 12px. This makes the base font size (1rem) 12px, which is below the recommended 16px minimum for body text, causing readability issues for many users without zooming.
issuesFound.fix: Line 241: font-size: 100%;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule sets outline: none but provides no visible alternative focus indicator. The outline-offset and border-radius properties alone do not create a perceivable focus ring. This removes keyboard focus visibility for all interactive elements site-wide, blocking keyboard-only users from tracking their position.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static with width: auto and height: auto, which fails to visually hide content. This means screen-reader-only text remains fully visible on screen, cluttering the visual interface. A correct sr-only implementation requires position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clip to hide visually while keeping content accessible.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em which compresses space between words. This significantly impairs readability for users with dyslexia, low vision, or cognitive disabilities who rely on adequate word separation to parse text content accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 485: word-spacing: 0em;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em which compresses space between characters. This reduces character distinctiveness and impairs readability for users with dyslexia, low vision, or cognitive disabilities who need adequate letter separation to distinguish characters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 486: letter-spacing: 0em;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform with perspective and rotate for 3D effects with transition on transform. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 982-986 is empty and does not disable these motion effects, which may cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use transform: translateY(-2px) on hover with transition on transform. While there is a prefers-reduced-motion override at line 1048, it only targets .feature-card:hover and does not disable the base transition property, meaning motion may still occur during other interactions.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards use transform: translateY(-3px) on hover with transition on transform. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) at line 1212 only targets .product-card:hover and does not fully disable the transform transition for all product card interactions.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards use transform: translateY(-2px) on hover with transition on transform. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) at line 1305 only targets .pricing-card:hover and does not fully disable the transform transition for all pricing card interactions.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: Stat items use transform: translateY(-2px) on hover with transition on transform. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) at line 1372 only targets .stat-item:hover and does not fully disable the transform transition for all stat item interactions.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons use transform: rotate() on the ::after pseudo-element for the chevron indicator with transition on transform. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) at line 1434 only targets the ::after transition but does not fully disable rotation animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1434: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; transform: rotate(90deg); } .faq-item dt button[aria-expanded="true"]::after { transform: rotate(90deg); } }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none removing underlines from all links. issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div with onClick lacks keyboard support

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, `overflow: visible`, and `clip: auto`, which keeps content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually. This fails to visually hide content while keeping it available to assistive technology, violating the intended purpose of the class and potentially exposing private information visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 310-320: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` pseudo-class sets `outline: none` without providing a visible alternative focus indicator (such as `box-shadow` or a replacement `outline`). This removes the default focus ring for keyboard users across all interactive elements, making it impossible for keyboard-only users to track their current focus position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 294-298: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the `@media (forced-colors: active)` block, `:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` without providing a high-contrast alternative. Users with forced colors enabled (high contrast mode) lose all focus indicators, making navigation impossible for keyboard users in this mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 384-385: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (20px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` class sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the button difficult or impossible to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 493: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (20px × 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-icon` class sets `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`, creating a touch target of only 20×20 pixels. This falls below the 24px minimum and significantly below the 44px recommended target size, creating barriers for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 507: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `input[type="checkbox"]` selector sets `width: 20px` and `height: 20px`, creating a touch target below the 24px minimum. This makes the checkbox difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which reduces the space between words. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and can cause text to overlap at certain zoom levels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 227: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a` selector sets `text-decoration: none` without providing an alternative visual indicator (such as `border-bottom`, `font-weight` change, or background highlight) to distinguish links from surrounding text. This relies solely on color to identify links, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 249: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` OR add `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }` if removing underlines from default state.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The `.section-header p` selector uses `color: var(--colour-text-secondary)` which resolves to `#aaaaaa` (light gray) against a white background (`#ffffff`). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.03:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 265: `.section-header p { color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }` (using #334155 which provides ~7:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for hero note text
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-note` selector uses `color: var(--colour-neutral-500)` which is `#64748b` against a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 4.05:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text at 16px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 718: `.hero-note { color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }` (using #334155 for ~7:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter section text
issuesFound.explanation: The `.newsletter-section > p` selector uses `color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8)` (white at 80% opacity) against a gradient background of approximately `#5588bb` (blue). The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 3.18:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1285: `.newsletter-section > p { color: #ffffff; ... }` (solid white for ~4.8:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` class includes `transform` in its transition properties (`transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)`), which causes spatial movement on state changes. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this transform animation for users who have requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 448: (add after line 448) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Base font size set below 16px

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` pseudo-class sets `outline: none` without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as a box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 349: `:focus-visible { outline: none; outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` selector removes the default outline with `outline: none` and does not define a replacement visual indicator such as box-shadow or border-color change. Interactive buttons become invisible to keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 493: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without a visible replacement, making the mobile menu button invisible to keyboard users when focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 638: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Form checkbox focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible` selector removes the outline with no alternative visual indicator, preventing keyboard users from seeing when the checkbox has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1543: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` class sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 544: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-icon` class sets `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited precision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 556: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]` selector sets `width: 20px` and `height: 20px`, which is smaller than the 24px minimum touch target size. Users may struggle to toggle the checkbox.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1541: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `clip: auto`, which keeps the content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 373: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which reduces the space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap at certain zoom levels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 323: `word-spacing: 0;` or `word-spacing: 0.05em;`

issuesFound.title: Transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` element has a `transition: transform` property (line 846) and transform effects on hover (line 852), but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block at line 858 is empty and does not disable these animations. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 858: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector uses `color: var(--colour-text-secondary)` which resolves to `#aaaaaa` on a white background (`#ffffff`), resulting in a contrast ratio of 2.8:1. This fails WCAG AA for normal text (4.5:1) and large text (3:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 13: `--colour-text-secondary: #767676;` (or darker)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for brand primary text
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `[id$="-submenu"] li a` uses `color: var(--colour-brand-primary)` which is `#5588bb` on white background, resulting in a 4.0:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG AA for normal text which requires 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3: `--colour-brand-primary: #3b6ca0;` (or darker)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for error text
issuesFound.explanation: The `.required-marker` class uses `color: var(--colour-error)` which is `#ee9999` on white background, resulting in a 2.1:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG AA for normal text (4.5:1) and large text (3:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 27: `--colour-error: #d32f2f;` (or darker)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in header navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The `#nav-menu > li > a` selector inherits `text-decoration: none` from the global `a` rule (line 328) and only provides a colour change on hover (line 670). Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text for users with colour vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 670: `#nav-menu > li > a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-info-light); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a` selector inherits `text-decoration: none` and only changes colour on hover (line 759), failing to provide a non-colour visual differentiator for links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 759: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside `@media (forced-colors: active)`, the `:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without providing a high-contrast alternative such as a thick border. This removes focus indicators for users in Windows High Contrast mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 362: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

fpTitles: Link underline removed without alternative in account navigation

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers continue to announce the button as expanded after the menu closes, causing confusion about the current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line in mobileNav.close() after `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`: Add `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens dropdown panels by removing the hidden attribute but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded state to users, failing WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line in dropdownMenu._openInstance() after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close() does not update aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion panel is hidden but the aria-expanded attribute remains "true" or unset, preventing screen reader users from knowing the panel has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line in siteSearch._close() after `_searchState.open = false;`: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() lacks screen reader announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the mobile navigation drawer without announcing the state change to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technologies are not informed that the menu has opened and that focus context has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line in mobileNav.open() before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', ...);`: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() lacks screen reader announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the menu has closed and that the interface has returned to the previous context.
issuesFound.fix: Line in mobileNav.close() before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);`: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() lacks live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count and visibility changes are not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match the selected filters or that the grid has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line in productFilter.applyFilters() after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() lacks live region announcement for billing change
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices visually but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed that prices have switched to monthly or annual billing until they navigate to the updated text.
issuesFound.fix: Line in pricing.setPeriod() after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator marks invalid fields without setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function adds error classes and displays error messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line in inlineValidator._renderResult() else block after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation passes, the function removes error styling but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid based on stale attribute values.
issuesFound.fix: Line in inlineValidator._renderResult() if block after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);`: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not activate focus trap or move focus
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the navigation drawer but does not move focus into the drawer or activate a focus trap. Keyboard users cannot access the navigation links without manually tabbing, and focus may be lost behind the modal overlay, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line in mobileNav.open() after `_lockScroll();`: Add focus trap activation: `if (!_trap) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); } _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the navigation drawer but does not return keyboard focus to the trigger button. The conditional deactivation of an uninitialized focus trap fails to restore focus context, causing keyboard users to lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line in mobileNav.close() after `_unlockScroll();`: Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked updates

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation panel lacks an aria-label prop. The button contains only the ✕ character, which may not be announced clearly or understood by screen reader users as a close action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2510: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for navigating to the previous testimonial slide lacks an aria-label prop. The button contains only the ‹ character, which does not provide sufficient context for screen reader users to understand its function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3760: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for navigating to the next testimonial slide lacks an aria-label prop. The button contains only the › character, which does not provide sufficient context for screen reader users to understand its function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3770: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label not programmatically associated with input
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component renders a label element that displays the label text but does not use the htmlFor prop to associate it with the input element passed as children. This breaks the programmatic relationship between the label and the form field, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the purpose of the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150:

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page uses a CSS class (nav-link--current) for visual styling, but lacks aria-current="page". This prevents screen reader users from identifying which page is the current page in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2440: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link indicating the current page uses a CSS class (mobile-nav__link--current) for visual styling, but lacks aria-current="page". This prevents screen reader users from identifying which page is the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2540: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk (*) used to visually indicate required fields is rendered as text content without aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce "asterisk" which may be confusing or redundant when the "(required)" text is also provided via VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: *

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop to indicate a loading state, but does not set aria-busy="true" on the button element. This prevents assistive technology from announcing the busy state to users while the action is being processed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element containing skip navigation links lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page (desktop, mobile, footer), this landmark cannot be uniquely identified by screen reader users when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1920: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary desktop navigation element lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. With multiple navigation regions present (skip links, mobile nav, footer nav), screen reader users cannot distinguish this specific navigation landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2420: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Desktop navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute misplaced on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal overlay div instead of the dialog div that has role="dialog". This incorrectly marks the overlay as modal instead of the dialog itself, potentially causing assistive technology to incorrectly handle focus and modality.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4360: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Line 4370: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div with onClick lacks button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the menu, but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers (Enter/Space). Keyboard users cannot activate this control to close the navigation, and screen reader users may not recognize it as interactive.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2490: <div role="button" aria-label="Close navigation menu" tabIndex={0} className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div with onClick lacks button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has an onClick handler to close the dialog, but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. While the dialog can be closed via Escape key, the click-outside-to-close functionality is not accessible to keyboard-only users who may need to access the overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4360: <div role="button" aria-label="Close dialog" tabIndex={0} className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }}>

fpTitles: MobileNav backdrop div with onClick lacks button semantics
Modal overlay div with onClick lacks button semantics

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to indicate error state but only applies a CSS class. It does not set `aria-invalid` on the input element, so screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1160: Add `aria-invalid={invalid || undefined}` to the input element props.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not map it to `aria-invalid`, preventing assistive technology from announcing the error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1174: Add `aria-invalid={invalid || undefined}` to the textarea element props.

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not set `aria-invalid` on the select element, failing to communicate error states to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1190: Add `aria-invalid={invalid || undefined}` to the select element props.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has `aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error"` when in error state, but the FormGroup wrapping it is not passed `errorId="newsletter-email-error"`, so the error message element is never rendered with that ID, creating a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2428: Add `errorId="newsletter-email-error"` prop to the FormGroup component wrapping the email input.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has `aria-describedby="contact-name-error"` but the FormGroup is not passed `errorId="contact-name-error"`, resulting in a broken ARIA reference when validation errors occur.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2520: Add `errorId="contact-name-error"` prop to the FormGroup wrapping the name input.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has `aria-describedby="contact-email-error"` but the FormGroup lacks the `errorId` prop, creating a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2538: Add `errorId="contact-email-error"` prop to the FormGroup wrapping the email input.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has `aria-describedby="contact-subject-error"` but the FormGroup lacks the `errorId` prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2556: Add `errorId="contact-subject-error"` prop to the FormGroup wrapping the subject input.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea has `aria-describedby` that includes 'contact-message-error' when in error state, but the FormGroup is not passed `errorId="contact-message-error"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2574: Add `errorId="contact-message-error"` prop to the FormGroup wrapping the message textarea.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing required accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an `aria-label` prop per its TypeScript interface, but ThemeToggle renders it without passing one. This results in an icon-only button with no accessible name, making it unreachable to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: Add `aria-label={LABELS[theme]}` to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button manages an open/close state (isOpen) and has aria-controls, but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the expanded/collapsed state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1290: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles section visibility (isExpanded) and has aria-controls, but lacks aria-expanded to convey the state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: Add `aria-expanded={isExpanded}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button toggles content visibility (isOpen) and has aria-controls, but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2330: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Links marked as current (item.current) receive visual styling but lack `aria-current="page"`, preventing screen readers from identifying the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1345: Add `aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}` to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Current page links in mobile navigation have visual styling but lack `aria-current="page"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: Add `aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}` to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons maintain a selected state (value prop) and visually indicate selection, but lack `aria-pressed` to communicate the pressed state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2090: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}` to the Monthly button. Line 2098: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}` to the Annual button.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons track an active state (activeFilter) and show visual selection, but lack `aria-pressed` to indicate the toggle state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to the button element inside the map.

issuesFound.title: Button component spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When loading, the button displays a spinner span that is purely decorative (loading text is provided via VisuallyHidden), but it lacks `aria-hidden="true"`, potentially causing redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1030: Change `<span className="btn-spinner" />` to `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual required field marker (*) is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" in addition to the "(required)" text provided via VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1140: Change ` *` to ` *`.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the navigation but lacks `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and keyboard event handlers, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1365: Add `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }}` to the div.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has onClick to close the dialog but lacks `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and keyboard support, violating keyboard accessibility requirements for interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750: Add `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }}` to the overlay div.

issuesFound.fix: Line 825-827: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove focus outline without a visible replacement. The negative outline-offset (-3px) does not create a visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 966: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove focus outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when a checkbox has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1040: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove focus outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focused footer links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1064-1066: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on footer nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove focus outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which footer link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1070-1072: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove focus outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which social link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1078-1080: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes focus outline without providing an alternative. In Windows High Contrast mode, keyboard focus becomes invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335-338: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid Highlight; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class does not hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which does NOT hide content from sighted users. Content meant to be screen-reader-only will be fully visible, defeating the purpose of the utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 317-325: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments will struggle to activate these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 437-441: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons have min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24×24 pixel minimum. Users with motor impairments cannot reliably activate these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 452-456: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24×24 pixel minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments cannot reliably select checkboxes.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em which reduces spacing between words. This can make text harder to read, particularly for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 107: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: All links have text-decoration: none applied globally, removing underlines. Without an alternative visual differentiator (border-bottom, background highlight, or distinct colour), links in body text cannot be distinguished from surrounding text, violating SC 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 145: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; } or add a default link colour: a { text-decoration: none; color: var(--colour-brand-primary); border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link has transform transition but the reduced-motion media query (lines 356-357) is empty and does not disable the animation. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 356-357: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: translateY(-200%); transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have transform transition but no reduced-motion override. The transform: translateY(1px) on :active and transform: translateY(-1px) on hover will still animate for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 422: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation override is empty for .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The reduced-motion media query for .hero-image img contains empty rules and does not disable the transform transition. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the 3D perspective animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 634-637: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Root font-size below 16px impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, making all rem-based sizes 12px instead of the standard 16px. Body text becomes 12px, below the recommended minimum for comfortable reading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 103: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 16px; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` without providing any visible alternative focus indicator (no box-shadow, border, or background change). This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements, making keyboard navigation impossible for sighted keyboard users. The forced-colors media query compounds this by also removing outline in high-contrast mode.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 322-324: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`
Line 333-336: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class does not hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, `overflow: visible`, and `clip: auto` — the opposite of what a screen-reader-only utility should do. This class makes content visible instead of hiding it visually, breaking the intended purpose of providing text for assistive technology while hiding it from sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346-355: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 20px` which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 (AA) minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments or using touch devices may struggle to activate this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 467: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` has a `transform` transition that creates spatial movement on hover, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block at lines 780-785 is empty and does not disable or override this animation. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 780-785: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em` which compresses word spacing globally. Negative word-spacing reduces the visual gaps between words, making text harder to read especially for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 288: `body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: `.stat-label` uses `color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3)` (white at 30% opacity) on a gradient background from `#5588bb` to `#bb88ee` (defined at lines 6 and 8). The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 1.4:1, far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text. Text is illegible for users with low vision or colour vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 705: `.stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes underlines from all links globally. For body text links, there is no alternative visual differentiator (no font-weight increase, border-bottom, or background highlight on hover) to distinguish links from surrounding text. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 as links are distinguished by colour alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 305: `a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; }` (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)

issuesFound.title: Base font-size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` rule sets `font-size: 12px` which becomes the root size for all rem units. With `--font-size-base: 1rem` (line 103), body text renders at 12px — below the recommended 16px minimum for comfortable reading. This affects readability for all users, particularly those with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 263: `html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 16px; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }`

fpTitles: Base font-size below recommended minimum

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule removes the outline (outline: none) without providing any visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users will have no visual indication of which element has focus, making navigation impossible for sighted keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 267: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.explanation: In Windows High Contrast Mode (forced-colors: active), the focus-visible outline is removed without replacement. Users relying on high contrast themes will have no visible focus indicator, completely blocking keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes content VISIBLE instead of hiding it visually. This is the opposite of the intended behavior—screen reader-only content will be visible to all users, breaking the skip-link and other accessibility features.
issuesFound.fix: Line 254-263: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.fix: Line 919: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links. Without an alternative visual differentiator (border-bottom, background highlight, or font-weight change), links are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour.
issuesFound.fix: Line 139: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; } a:hover { text-decoration-thickness: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience transform animations on button interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 288: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation has empty reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform transitions and hover transforms, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 739 contains empty rules that do not actually disable the motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 739: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: CTA button transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary includes transform in its transition and :hover state (translateY), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience transform animations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 677: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load. Screen reader users have no way to know the initial collapsed state of the navigation.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen reader users have no indication of the initial collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel.init() does not initialize aria-pressed on play button
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button does not have aria-pressed initialized to "false" on page load. Screen reader users have no indication of the initial paused state.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter updates the visible count of products, the change is not announced to screen readers. Users cannot perceive that the number of visible products has changed.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed (monthly/annual), the price change is not announced to screen readers. Users cannot perceive that prices have been updated.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions toggle CSS classes and manage focus traps, but never update aria-expanded on the hamburger trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce whether the navigation drawer is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2680: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`
  Line 2700: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not set initial aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button's aria-expanded state is not initialized on page load. The drawer starts closed, but the trigger has no aria-expanded attribute, leaving screen readers unaware of the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2740: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions toggle panel visibility but never set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce whether dropdown menus are expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2880: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 2890: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not set initial aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers receive aria-haspopup="true" but not aria-expanded="false" during registration. The initial collapsed state is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2980: add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger, but open() never sets aria-expanded="true". This leaves the trigger in an incorrect state after opening an accordion item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4720: add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation error handling adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen readers cannot programmatically identify which fields have validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid after being invalid, the markValid() function removes the error description but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove it. The field remains marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2110: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` at the start of the function body.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) update CSS active classes but never set aria-pressed. Screen readers cannot announce which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4150: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons update CSS active classes but never set aria-pressed. Screen readers cannot announce which filter category is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3850: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function hides the dropdown panel and emits an event but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot tell that the dropdown has closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init() function registers dropdowns but never sets the initial aria-expanded="false" state on trigger buttons. Screen readers have no way to know the initial collapsed state of dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2638: `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function sets item.open to true and adds CSS classes but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot tell that the accordion panel has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4186: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState() does not update aria-pressed on toggle buttons

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() does not update aria-pressed on filter buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles CSS classes on filter buttons but never sets aria-pressed to indicate which filter is active. Screen reader users cannot tell which product category is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3896: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine that the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1946: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but never clears aria-invalid from the field. Screen reader users continue to hear that the field is invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1959: `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not announce state changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and close() functions emit custom events but do not announce the navigation state change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the mobile navigation opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not announce state changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions emit custom events but do not announce the dropdown state change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when dropdowns open or close.

issuesFound.title: productFilter does not announce filter changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid and emits an event but does not announce the filter change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed how many products match the current filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3880: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(activeCategory) || STRINGS.announce.filterAll);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates prices and emits an event but does not announce the billing period change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the billing period switches between monthly and annual.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component accepts an `htmlFor` prop but never applies it to the `<label>` element. Without `htmlFor`, screen readers cannot associate the label with its form field, and clicking the label will not focus the input. This fails WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~680: `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button has `aria-haspopup="true"` and `aria-controls` but is missing `aria-expanded`. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is open or closed. This fails WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~360: `aria-expanded={isOpen}`

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button has `aria-controls` pointing to the answer panel but lacks `aria-expanded`. Screen reader users cannot tell if the answer is revealed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1105: `aria-expanded={isOpen}`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter buttons visually indicate active state via CSS classes but lack `aria-pressed` to communicate the selected filter to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~838: `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle renders an icon-only button without passing the required `aria-label` prop to IconButton. The `LABELS` constant is defined but never used. Screen reader users hear only the icon character with no context.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~535: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal placed on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: `aria-modal="true"` is placed on the backdrop overlay element instead of the element with `role="dialog"`. Screen readers may not properly constrain focus to the dialog content.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1305: Remove `aria-modal="true"` from overlay div. Line ~1310: Add `aria-modal="true"` to the dialog div: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId}`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Links with `item.current === true` receive a CSS class indicating current page status but lack `aria-current="page"`. Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~405: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation links with `item.current === true` visually indicate current page but lack `aria-current="page"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~500: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks navigation is a landmark region without an accessible name. When multiple nav elements exist on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this nav from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~285: `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation has an `id` but no `aria-label`. Screen reader users cannot distinguish this nav from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~395: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation body uses `<nav>` without an accessible name, making it indistinguishable from other nav landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~480: `<nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">`

issuesFound.title: Button missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When `isLoading` is true, the button becomes disabled but does not communicate the busy state via `aria-busy`. Screen reader users are not informed that an operation is in progress.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~235: `aria-busy={isLoading}`

issuesFound.title: Button spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span `<span className="btn-spinner" />` is decorative (the button already announces loading via VisuallyHidden) but lacks `aria-hidden="true"`, potentially announcing meaningless content to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~240: `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: Required field asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk ` *` is decorative (a VisuallyHidden "(required)" label follows) but lacks `aria-hidden="true"`. Screen readers may announce "asterisk" redundantly.

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid when in error state

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid when in error state

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid when in error state
